title: salicaceae
intro: The Salicaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as willow and poplar families. This family consists of approximately 200 species, primarily found in the Northern Hemisphere, with a mix of deciduous and evergreen trees and shrubs. Members of the Salicaceae family are known for their distinctive leaves, often with a serrated or toothed edge, and their ability to thrive in a variety of environments. The Salicaceae family includes many economically important species, such as willows used for erosion control and paper production.
